events.title: US claims of Cuba’s ‘unique threat’ jar with reality of hunger and blackouts
events.description: State department’s 100-page report is latest push in Trump administration campaign to bring down government The US government’s latest allegations that Cuba is behind “many of the most significant upheavals in recent American political history” and that its leaders pose “a unique threat to American homeland security” have come as startling revelations to those living under the six-month-long US oil blockade in Havana, where desperate people search through piles of uncollected rubbish for food, or else swelter at home in 24-hour-plus blackouts. The unsigned 100-page report, Cuba: Capital of 21st-Century Communism, published by the US state department on Monday, blames Havana for everything from “the George Floyd riots to the rise of antifa to the explosion of pro-terrorist activism on American college campuses”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 US claims of Cuba’s ‘unique threat’ jar with reality of hunger and blackouts

events.title: People with secret ‘safe phones’ advised to turn off devices during national test of AusAlert emergency warning
events.description: Ten-second ‘siren’ to blare on most phones on Monday as part of national test despite reservations from some states Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An unsilenceable siren will blare out of most phones in Australia on Monday afternoon for 10 seconds, with the federal government confirming a national test of the new AusAlert emergency alert system will go ahead, despite reservations from some state governments. Domestic violence campaigners and the National Emergency Management Agency have also repeated advice that people who may have secret “safe phones”, kept hidden for personal safety reasons, may want to turn off their devices for up to 24 hours after the test scheduled for 2pm (AEST) on Monday. iPhones 11 and up running iOS 26.4 or later; Apple Watch SE2, SE3, Series 6 and up running watchOS 26.4 or later; Android devices running Android 12 or later. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 People with secret ‘safe phones’ advised to turn off devices during national test of AusAlert emergency warning

events.title: We received no alert, say leaders of First Nations communities razed by Canadian wildfires
events.description: Chiefs in Ontario say they were left to self-evacuate before homes burned to ground, after being told there was no imminent threat Fast-moving wildfires have burned two First Nations communities in Canada to the ground, as members said they were left to self-evacuate without government help or emergency alerts. Thirteen communities in Ontario province have been ordered to evacuate because of wildfires, most of them First Nations. The Ontario premier, Doug Ford, told reporters on Monday that close to 2,000 people had been evacuated so far to cities including Toronto and Niagara Falls. Continue reading...

events.title: Zelensky faces deepening political crisis as protests dig in
events.description: In Ukraine, protesters say they’re giving Volodymyr Zelensky until Friday to re-instate the popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, and fire General Oleksandr Syrsky, the military chief of staff. The President removed Fedorov from his post last week, citing a clash between the two men. The protests have brought Syrsky’s unpopularity to the forefront. Through credited with stopping the Russian invaders at the gates of Kyiv in 2022, he’s since gained a reputation for brutality, and for wasting lives unnecessarily. He's also, as our correspondent Gulliver Cragg reports, accused of tolerance for corruption.

events.title: Joseph Aoun in Washington: Bilal Saab firmly believes 'this meeting couldn't have gone any better'
events.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Bilal Saab, Senior Managing Director of the Trends Research Group and a close friend of Lebanese President Joseph Aoun. Mr. Saab offers a cautiously optimistic assessment of a pivotal moment in Lebanese diplomacy, arguing that President Aoun's visit to Washington represents an important opportunity to consolidate international backing for Lebanon's sovereignty and state institutions. While acknowledging the formidable structural challenges posed by Hezbollah, regional tensions, and extremely complex Israeli-Lebanese security challenges, Mr. Saab contends that success will not come from military coercion, but rather a focus on the gradual strengthening of the Lebanese state. According to Mr. Saab, Lebanon is both a testing ground for American regional diplomacy and a fragile experiment in state-building that will require sustained political support and not symbolic declarations.

events.title: Shanghai science forum photos show China's AI, robotics advances in rivalry with US
events.description: China's leading technology companies showcased hundreds of cutting-edge products at the World AI Conference in Shanghai — from advanced robotics to artificial intelligence systems. The nation's annual flagship AI event, which was held from July 17 to 20, took place against the backdrop of a heated U.S.-China tech rivalry. More than 1,100 companies participated. Chinese companies have developed more than 400 humanoid robot products, more than half the world’s total, state media said. Open-source AI models from China are increasingly challenging leading American ones as they are more widely adopted globally. ___ This is a photo gallery curated by AP photo editors.

events.title: Australia news live: Victoria police report rise in antisemitic incidents since October 2023
events.description: Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The New South Wales premier, Chris Minns , says the state’s Labor party is under threat from One Nation and “we’ve got to fight for those votes”. Appearing on ABC Radio National this morning, Minns was asked if One Nation was a “racist party”. He said people who had concerns about the levels of immigration weren’t necessarily racist but some of the things Pauline Hanson had said were. Certainly some of the things Pauline Hanson says are definitely racist, when she said there’s no such thing as a good Muslim. I mean, how else do you characterise that? You’ve got to be straight with people. And that clearly is. But I don’t think everyone who’s supporting them currently is racist at all … And we’ve got to fight for those votes. I think that we are under threat in a whole bunch of seats as a result of the surge of votes for One Nation … So we’re doing some what we regard as practical commonsense changes to policy in New South Wales. When people join the Do Not Call Register or unsubscribe from marketing messages, they are making a clear choice. Those choices must be respected – especially given the heightened risks of financial loss and psychological harm from gambling marketing. The scale and range of these breaches point to serious weaknesses in TAB’s compliance systems. Continue reading...

events.title: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat
events.description: In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heat Japan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. The Japan Meteorological Agency coined the new designation to warn the public of the heat dangers of days that were once freak weather events but have now become more common. The central Japan cities of Gujo and Tajimi in Gifu prefecture, along with Toyota in Aichi prefecture, all hit 40C. Gujo’s 40C and Toyota’s 40.1C were all-time highs recorded at their respective weather stations. Continue reading...

events.title: ‘We are preserving a tradition’: how Ghana’s sensationalist film posters became collectible art
events.description: Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt duped Sitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully on to his canvas – a flour sack – adding blood to a knife being wielded by a man. Higher on the canvas, he had started on an outline of a skull. Heavy J was creating a poster, but not as you might have expected for a horror film. Instead, it was for the animated fairytale The Little Mermaid. The man with the knife was not a killer but the film’s kind-hearted prince, Eric. The skull was also unrelated to the story. “We add more to make people interested,” said Heavy J, whose real name is Jeaurs Affutu. Continue reading...

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows

events.title: ‘Business as usual is a business of death’: UN adopts road safety pledge over US objection
events.description: The UN General Assembly closed its first ever High-level Meeting on road safety on Tuesday by adopting a sweeping new declaration aimed at halving road deaths by 2030 – over the sole objection of the United States.

events.title: Brittany Higgins accuses Pauline Hanson of victim-blaming for calling domestic violence a ‘two-way street’
events.description: One Nation leader criticised by advocates after telling women being abused: ‘don’t put up with it’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Pauline Hanson has described domestic violence as “wrong” but a “two-way street”, and that women being abused should not put up with it, drawing condemnation from advocates who have accused the One Nation leader of victim-blaming. On Wednesday, Hanson told radio station 4BC domestic violence in Australia was a scourge but that society was under “so much pressure”, when asked to call out the issue. Continue reading...

events.title: India's youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown
events.description: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.

events.title: Germany records rise in sexual violence against children
events.description: Germany's Criminal Police Office has reported over 17,000 cases of sexual violence against children in the past year. Organizations such as Zartbitter provide support to victims.

events.title: UN refugee agency reports 144 migrants dead or missing off Mauritanian coast
events.description: The UN’s refugee agency (UNHCR) reports that 144 migrants attempting to reach Europe are dead or missing due to a series of “tragic maritime incidents” off the coast of Mauritania last week.

events.title: American tourists stabbed outside Athens' Acropolis as police detain suspect in apparent random attack
events.description: A 60-year-old Greek man allegedly suffering from psychological problems stabbed two American visitors near the Acropolis Museum in Athens, officials said.

events.title: Meet the women breaking world records in lumberjack sports
events.description: The BBC finds out what is driving the sports' increasing popularity with women and what it takes to be the best.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
events.description: Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials. According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora. Continue reading...

events.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires his military chief after protests and names a new one
events.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi as commander-in-chief of Ukraine's armed forces following days of protests demanding his removal. Zelenskyy appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new army chief.

events.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires military chief after days of protests
events.description: Mass protests in recent days in Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities had called for the removal of commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi. Volodymyr Zelenskyy has named Mykhailo Drapatyi as the army's new chief.

events.title: Fact check: Trump wasn't edited out of Spain World Cup photo
events.description: After Spain won the FIFA World Cup, the Spanish Football Federation posted a photo of the trophy celebrations, raising questions about whether Donald Trump had been edited out. We took a closer look.

events.title: Tour de France: Evenepoel wins time trial to strengthen second overall
events.description: Remco Evenepoel won the individual time trial on Stage 16 of the Tour de France, 2 days after his victory at Plateau de Solaison. The three-time world time trial champion finished 28 seconds ahead of Tadej Pogacar and 1'04'' clear of Mattias Skjelmose, between Évian and Thonon. The Belgian remains second overall, 4'32'' behind the Slovenian in the yellow jersey.

events.title: Canada evaluating 'all options' after fresh US tariffs threat
events.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said Tuesday he was looking at "all options" after US President Donald Trump made plans to impose new 50 percent tariffs on many Canadian goods in 30 days. Carney also said that he and Trump had agreed to "intensify discussions" in the coming weeks on a possible deal, but did not suggest he would travel to Washington for any talks. FRANCE 24's Christopher Guly reports from Ottawa.

events.title: Zelensky fires army chief Oleksandr Syrsky, ‌a 'victory for protestors'
events.description: President ​Volodymyr ​Zelensky said on Tuesday that Major ​General ‌Mykhailo ⁠Drapaty ‌will take over as Ukraine's ⁠commander-in-chief after firing Soviet-trained top commander Oleksandr Syrsky. "This is a victory for the protestors who took to the streets a week ago, when Zelensky fired Fedorov," says FRANCE 24 correspondent Gulliver Cragg. "Their main demand was that Syrsky be fired." He added that "Drapaty was the one Fedorov wanted to see in that position to replace Syrsky".

events.title: Biodiversity fears as human-bred hybrid fish integrate into Philippines lake
events.description: Escaped flowerhorn cichlids are causing concern for native species and about parasites capable of infecting humans Escaped ornamental aquarium fish have integrated into a local ecosystem in the Philippines, but scientists say they may be threatening the native biodiversity of the lake. Flowerhorn cichlids – human-bred hybrid fish prized for their bright-gold colour and prominent head humps – are believed to have escaped from breeding facilities into Lake Sampaloc, which sits in a volcanic crater, during a typhoon. Continue reading...

events.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
events.description: Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters. The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in which a court has ordered the work to be ⁠suspended. Construction continued, however, according to US officials and satellite imagery reviewed by Reuters. Continue reading...

events.title: Iran-backed Houthis announce blockade. And, Cyclospora source remains unknown

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: ICE arrests human rights lawyer who fled Chinese crackdown
events.description: Arrest in Pennsylvania of Wu Shaoping, who is awaiting asylum decision, raises fears of deportation and persecution A Chinese human rights lawyer has been arrested by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), raising concerns he could be deported to China where he would face persecution. Wu Shaoping fled China at the end of 2019 amid a crackdown on human rights lawyers. He travelled to the US on a tourist visa and made an asylum claim in 2020, for which he is still awaiting a decision. Continue reading...

The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Australia spent more than $9m for each asylum seeker held on Nauru last year – so why can’t they afford to buy food?
events.description: Many of the more than 100 people awaiting processing on the tiny Pacific island are skipping meals to make ends meet, a new survey has found Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Most refugees and asylum seekers held in Nauru’s detention centres say they cannot afford to buy enough food or access clean drinking water, despite the Australian government spending the equivalent of more than $9m per person last financial year. The group of more than 100 people have been sent indefinitely to the tiny Pacific island while they wait for their asylum claims to be processed. Many of the cohort say they are skipping meals to make ends meet, according to a new survey by the Asylum Seeker Resource Centre (ASRC) advocacy group. Continue reading...

events.title: Alleged rape victim ‘could have done a lot better’ if she had made up claims against well-connected Melbourne man, court told
events.description: Prosecutor tells court that ‘gaps or inconsistencies’ in the woman’s account ‘enhances her credibility’, but the defence maintains she is lying Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A woman who accused a well-connected Melbourne man of rape “could have done a much better job” if she was inventing the story for money, a Melbourne court has heard. But the accused’s lawyer has described the prosecution’s case as a “horrendous mixture” of “lies, fabrications, inconsistencies, [and] evidence that has been found to be demonstrably untrue”. Continue reading...

events.description: Powerball ticket sales in the United Kingdom will begin Tuesday, giving players across the Atlantic a chance at winning the same massive jackpot that U.S. players already play for.

events.title: Turkey-PKK talks: Is peace finally possible?
events.description: It was a year ago that PKK fighters held a symbolic disarmament ceremony, burning their weapons. Peace talks between the PKK and Turkey are going slowly, but some observers believe that they will succeed this time.

events.title: Can VR help you achieve your goals?
events.description: A Max Planck researcher has developed VR and smartphone tools that let you "meet" your future self. The science behind it is more powerful than it sounds.

events.title: Ousted Turkey opposition leader  plans to create a new party
events.description: Turkey's main opposition CHP faces a potentially historic rupture after months of infighting over its leadership.

events.title: DW correspondent Alican Uludag re-arrested in Turkey
events.description: The latest arrest comes two months after he was released from detention on charges including "insulting" President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.

events.title: 'Antifa' song climbs to top of charts after TV show cancellation
events.description: German rapper Danger Dan and pianist Igor Levitt had been invited to play a song for the 100th episode of a political satire show on TV. The performance was canceled at the last minute due to its message.

events.title: Louvre gallery where jewellery heist took place to reopen to public
events.description: The Apollo Gallery at Paris's Louvre Museum, the site of the jewelry heist of October 2025, is set to reopen to the public on Wednesday without any jewelry on display, an informed source told AFP.

events.title: Trump announces 100% tariffs on imported generic pharmaceutical drugs
events.description: US President Donald Trump announced a 100% tariff on imported generic pharmaceutical drugs on Tuesday. The tariff, which aims to bolster US pharmaceutical production, is set to go into effect in August 2028.

events.title: Senegal makes U-turn to back ex-president Sall for UN chief
events.description: In tonight's edition, a deadly ambush in northern Mali leaves at least 50 soldiers dead. Also, Nigeria is facing a new challenge in its fight against jihadist insurgents as foreign fighters join armed groups. And Senegalese President Bassirou Diomaye Faye is backing former president Macky Sall's bid to become UN Secretary-General.

events.title: Outrage in France as Macron appoints senator who fought gay marriage to key rights job
events.description: French lawmakers on Tuesday narrowly approved President Emmanuel Macron’s candidate to serve as Défenseur des droits (human rights ombdusman), despite vehement opposition from groups arguing that Senator François-Noël Buffet’s views run counter to the mission of defending the rights of the most vulnerable.

events.title: Evenepoel beats Pogacar by seconds in stage 16 of Tour de France
events.description: Olympic champion Remco Evenepoel powered to victory in the Tour de France's stage 16 time trial on Tuesday, beating overall leader Tadej Pogacar by 28 seconds to trim the Slovenian's advantage.

events.title: Burnham's 'cost of living government' | Zelenskyy sacks head of armed forces
events.description: Tap to never miss an episode: Follow Cheat Sheet here

events.title: Parents urged to stop buying  e-scooters as 'toys' as Ireland brings in new age limit
events.description: Ireland's deputy prime minister has urged parents to stop buying electric scooters as "toys" for their children ahead of new age limits coming into force.

events.title: Why last two years are worrying for future of Arctic
events.description: Arctic winter sea ice is back in rapid decline after a slowdown in melting at the start of the decade, scientists have found.

events.title: 'One in 15 million chance' identical quadruplets born in Australia
events.description: An Australian woman has given birth to identical quadruplets conceived naturally from a single embryo - in a one in 15 million chance occurrence.

events.title: Descendant of Nazi opponent tries to remove far-right party's wreath at Hitler plot memorial event
events.description: A descendant of an executed opponent of the Nazis tried to remove a wreath from the far-right Alternative for Germany party at an event in Berlin marking the 82nd anniversary of the failed attempt to kill Nazi dictator Adolf Hitler
events.ticker_label: 💥 Descendant of Nazi opponent tries to remove far-right party's wreath at Hitler plot memorial event

events.title: Hungarian prosecutors raid Orbán party's data center in embezzlement probe
events.description: Prosecutors in Hungary have raided offices housing data servers used by former Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party

events.title: UK leader Burnham gathers his Cabinet after sweeping away Starmer supporters
events.description: New UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham is gathering his Cabinet after removing allies of predecessor Keir Starmer from the government

events.title: 2,000 volunteers join a painstaking effort to restore massive prehistoric skeleton
events.description: More than 2,000 volunteers are helping Polish scientists restore a Mastodonsaurus skeleton

events.title: Ukraine overhauls its military top brass for the fight against Russia
events.description: Ukraine is continuing its fight against Russia with new military leadership

events.title: Turkish deposed opposition leader plans to create a new party to take on Erdogan
events.description: The deposed leader of Turkey's main opposition has announced plans to form a new political party to challenge President Recep Tayyip Erdogan

events.title: Two of Puerto Rico's top government officials to step down as corruption allegations persist
events.description: Puerto Rico’s governor says that her embattled chief of staff, Francisco Domenech, and deputy chief of staff, Itza García, are stepping down next month as they deny corruption allegations

events.title: Baristas behind bars: Thai prisoners brew a path back to daily life
events.description: It looks like a regular coffee shop, albeit set up in an old red bus. Stairs lead up to a serving hatch, with a menu of drink options. A selection of jaunty coffee-related messages like “My one and only love: Americano” and “Miss you a latte,” adorn the side of the bus.

events.title: Alleged 'mime' stalker stripped to underwear, covered himself in cooking oil: officials
events.description: Massachusetts man Hernan Villagran Lux allegedly stripped to his underwear and slathered cooking oil on his body near female park workers before collapsing in court.

events.title: California toddler bitten by rattlesnake hiding beneath Amazon package amid active snake season
events.description: A 3-year-old girl was bitten by a rattlesnake hiding beneath an Amazon package outside her California home and spent two days receiving antivenom.

events.title: Fleeing suspect captured after wild Range Rover chase across Los Angeles County
events.description: Video shows the black Range Rover weaving through traffic on the 101 Freeway before CHP officers rammed the SUV to end the chase in Montebello.

events.title: Florida man arrested in IHOP restaurant after police say he stabbed a rideshare driver
events.description: Jovaughn Nathan Matthie was arrested in the middle of an IHOP restaurant after police say he stabbed a rideshare driver nearby. He has been charged with attempted murder.

events.title: Congress to put American Bar Association in hot seat over DEI rules tied to law school accreditation
events.description: Rep. Brandon Gill, R-Texas, called a hearing on Wednesday to investigate DEI's influence on law schools and the profession. The top witness is Michelle A. Behnke, the president of the American Bar Association.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Congress to put American Bar Association in hot seat over DEI rules tied to law school accreditation

events.title: Lindsey Graham's sister breaks from foreign policy playbook with a different agenda and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: 'America's Most Wanted' fugitive laid blueprint for vanishing before 20-year escape: affidavit
events.description: Ronald Fischer emailed his attorney, canceled a London flight and studied identity-change literature in the days before vanishing during his 2005 trial, according to an affidavit.
events.ticker_label: 💥 'America's Most Wanted' fugitive laid blueprint for vanishing before 20-year escape: affidavit

events.title: $290M cocaine haul found ‘skillfully concealed’ in massive banana shipment
events.description: Italian officials said the 1,700-pound haul of cocaine, hidden among banana pallets, could have generated roughly $290 million on the street.

events.title: Sinaloa Cartel boss 'El Mayo' sentenced to life, ordered to pay $15B in drug trafficking profits
events.description: Sinaloa Cartel co-founder Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada Garcia received a life sentence and was ordered to forfeit $15 billion in drug trafficking profits.

events.title: Andy Burnham takes over as UK prime minister, calls for 'new political model'
events.description: Andy Burnham enters Downing Street vowing to shift power away from London, but his past criticism of Trump could create early friction with Washington.

events.title: Steve Forbes urges Trump to keep the Strait of Hormuz open at all costs
events.description: Publisher Steve Forbes calls for maximum pressure on Tehran regime, urges Trump administration to keep the Strait of Hormuz open as a non-negotiable imperative.

events.title: Trump administration touts more than $1B in UN budget cuts as reform push gains momentum
events.description: The Trump administration says it secured over $1 billion in U.N. budget cuts in six months, including a $550 million peacekeeping budget reduction.
